original_hypothesis.title: Owner Can Drain All User-Claimable TITN via Unrestricted withdraw Function
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The withdraw function allows the owner to transfer any amount of any ERC20 token from the MergeTgt contract, including TITN designated for user claims. There is no check against outstanding user balances (totalTitnClaimable), no restriction on withdrawing the TITN token, and no timelock. Owner can drain all TITN while users hold non-zero claimableTitnPerUser, causing all subsequent claimTitn and withdrawRemainingTitn calls to revert.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: withdraw(titn, titn.balanceOf(address(this))) drains everything. The contract's solvency invariant (balance >= totalTitnClaimable) is completely unprotected from owner actions. This is not a bug in logic but a missing safeguard — the withdraw function should restrict token withdrawal to amounts not committed to users.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function withdraw(IERC20 token, uint256 amount) external onlyOwner { token.safeTransfer(owner(), amount); } — no check against totalTitnClaimable

preconditions: Owner account is compromised or acts maliciously
Users have pending claimable TITN in the contract

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Unrestricted withdraw of any token including user-committed TITN
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: No guard like require(token != titn || amount <= balance - totalTitnClaimable)
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No restriction on withdrawing committed TITN; no timelock; no multi-sig

analysis_questions: Is the owner a multi-sig or EOA?
Should withdraw restrict TITN to surplus (balance - totalTitnClaimable)?
Is there a timelock contract wrapping the owner?
validation_requirements: Verify claimTitn reverts for users with positive claimable
Verify withdrawRemainingTitn also fails post-360 days

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised owner calls withdraw(titn, titn.balanceOf(address(this))). All TITN drained to owner address. Users with claimableTitnPerUser > 0 call claimTitn → safeTransfer reverts (insufficient balance). After 360 days, withdrawRemainingTitn also reverts due to underflow. All user claims permanently blocked.
prerequisites: Owner key compromise or malicious owner

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Up to 173.7M TITN — complete protocol fund loss
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users who deposited TGT and have pending TITN claims
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: None via contract — requires legal/social recovery

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test verifying withdraw restrictions on committed TITN

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Immediately visible via TITN balance change

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
